pi_items.source: OpenSIS-Classic GitHub repository indicates student field categories are stored in 'student_field_categories' table (https://github.com/OS4ED/openSIS-Classic/blob/master/modules/users/Profiles.php). However, the task scripts query 'custom_field_categories' which does not exist in the standard OpenSIS schema.

pi_items.verified_value: The custom_fields table has columns: id (INT PRIMARY KEY), type (varchar), search (varchar), title (varchar), sort_order (numeric), select_options (varchar), category_id (numeric), system_field (char), required (varchar), default_selection (varchar), hide (varchar), last_updated (timestamp), updated_by (varchar)

privileged_info_summary: OpenSIS v9.2 is an open-source Student Information System with a LAMP stack (Linux, Apache, MariaDB, PHP). The task uses database credentials user='opensis_user', password='opensis_password_123', database='opensis'. OpenSIS stores custom field type information in a type column that accepts values like 0 (Text), 1 (Text Area), 2 (Select), and string representations like 'text', 'textarea', 'text box'. The task scripts reference 'custom_field_categories' table, but OpenSIS actually uses 'student_field_categories' for student field categories according to the official OpenSIS schema. Field names 'Transportation' and 'Bus Route Number' are user-specified task requirements, not facts about the OpenSIS system itself.
